verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Jesus answered them, and said, Verily, verily, I say unto you, Ye seek Me, not because ye saw the miracles but because ye did eat of the loaves and were filled.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Labor not for the meat which perisheth, but for that meat which endureth unto everlasting life.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: The mild and gentle is not always useful, but there are times when the teacher needs sharper language.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For if the disciple be dull and gross, then, in order to touch his dullness to the quick, we must rouse him with a goad.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And this the Son of God hath done in the present as well as in many other cases.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For when the crowds had come and found Jesus, and were flattering Him, and saying, “Master, when camest Thou hither?” to show that He desireth not honor from men, but looketh to one thing only, their salvation, He answereth them sharply, wishing to correct them not in this way only, but also by revealing and exposing their thoughts.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For what saith He?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Verily, verily, I say unto you,” (speaking positively and with a confirmation,) “Ye seek Me, not because ye saw miracles, but because ye did eat of the loaves and were filled.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: He chideth and reproveth them by these words, yet doth not so abruptly or violently, but very sparingly.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For He saith not, “O ye gluttons and belly-slaves, I have wrought so many wonders, and ye never have either followed Me, or marveled at My doings”; but mildly and gently somewhat in this manner; “Ye seek Me, not because ye saw miracles, but because ye did eat of the loaves and were filled”; speaking not only of the past, but also of the present miracle.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “It was not,” He saith, “the miracle of the loaves that astonished you, but the being filled.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And that He said not this of them by conjecture they straightway showed, for on this account they came the second time, as being about to enjoy the same (food) as before.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Wherefore they said, “Our fathers did eat manna in the wilderness.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Again they draw Him to (the subject of) carnal food, which was the chief accusation and charge against them.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But He stoppeth not at rebukes, but addeth instruction also, saying, “Labor not for the meat which perisheth, but for that meat which endureth unto everlasting life.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Which the Son of Man giveth unto you; for Him hath God the Father sealed.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: What He saith, is of this kind: “Make ye no account of this earthly, but of that spiritual food.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But since some of those who desire to live in doing nothing have abused this speech, as though Christ would entirely abolish working, it is seasonable to say somewhat to them.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For they slander, so to speak, all Christianity, and cause it to be ridiculed on the score of idleness.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: First however, we must mention that saying of Paul.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: What saith he?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Remember the Lord, how He said, It is more blessed to give than to receive.” ( Acts xx.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Now how can it be possible for him to give who hath not?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: How then saith Jesus to Martha, “Thou art careful and troubled about many things, but one thing is needful, and Mary hath chosen that good part”? ( Luke x.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 41, 42 ); and again, “Take no thought for the morrow.” ( Matt. vi.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For it is necessary now to resolve all these questions, not only that we may check men if they would be idle, but also that the oracles of God may not appear to bring in what is contradictory.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Now Paul in another place saith, “But we beseech you, brethren, that ye increase more and more, that ye study to be quiet, and to do your own business; that ye may walk honestly toward them that are without” ( 1 Thess. iv.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 10, 11, 12 ); and again; “Let him that stole, steal no more; but rather let him labor, working with his own hands, that he may have to give to him that needeth.” ( Eph. iv.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Here the Apostle bids not simply “work,” but to work so vigorously and laboriously, as to have thereby somewhat to give to others.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And in another place the same saith again; “These hands have ministered to my necessities, and to them that were with me.” ( Acts xx.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And writing to the Corinthians he said, “What is my reward then?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Verily, that when I preach the Gospel, I may make the Gospel of Christ without charge.” ( 1 Cor. ix.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And when he was in that city, he abode with Aquila and Priscilla, “and wrought, for by their occupation they were tentmakers.” ( Acts xviii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: These passages show a yet more decided opposition as to the letter; we must therefore now bring forward the solution.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: What then must be our reply?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: That to “take no thought,” doth not mean “not to work,” but “not to be nailed to the things of this life”; that is, to take no care for to-morrow’s ease, but to deem that superfluous.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For a man may do no work, and (yet) lay up treasure for the morrow; and a man may work, yet be careful for nothing; for carefulness and work are not the same thing; it is not as trusting to his work that a man worketh, but, “that he may impart to him that needeth.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And that too which was said to Martha refers not to works and working, but to this, that it is our duty to know the right season, and not to spend on carnal things the time proper for listening.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Thus Christ spake not the words as urging her to “idleness,” but to rivet her to listening.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “I came,” saith He, “to teach you needful things, but thou art anxious about a meal.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Dost thou desire to receive Me, and to provide for Me a costly table?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Provide another sort of entertainment, by giving me a ready hearing, and by imitating thy sister’s longing for instruction.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: He said not this to forbid her hospitality, (away with the thought! how could that be?) but to show that she ought not in the season for listening be busy about other matters.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For to say, “Labor not for the meat that perisheth,” is not the expression of one implying that we ought to be idle; (in fact, this most especially is “meat that perisheth,” for idleness is wont to teach all wickedness;) but that we ought to work, and to impart.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: This is meat that never perisheth; but if any be idle and gluttonous, and careth for luxury, that man worketh for “the meat that perisheth.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: So too, if a man by his labor should feed Christ, and give Him drink, and clothe Him, who so senseless and mad as to say that such an one labors for the meat that perisheth, when there is for this the promise of the kingdom that is to come, and of those good things?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: This meat endureth forever.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But at that time, since the multitudes made no account of filth, nor sought to learn who it was that did these things, and by what power, but desired one thing only, to fill their bellies without working; Christ with good reason called such food, “meat that perisheth.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “I fed,” He saith, “your bodies, that after this ye might seek that other food which endureth, which nourisheth the soul; but ye again run after that which is earthy.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Therefore ye do not understand that I lead you not to this imperfect food, but to that which giveth not temporal but eternal life, which nourisheth not the body but the soul.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Then when He had uttered such great words concerning Himself, and had said that He would give this food, in order that what was spoken might not stand in their way, to make His saying credible He attributeth the supply to the Father.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For after saying, “Which the Son of Man shall give you”; He addeth, “Him hath God the Father sealed,” that is, “hath sent Him for this purpose, that He might bring the food to you.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: The saying also admits of another interpretation; for in another place Christ saith, “He that heareth My words, hath set to his seal that God is true” ( c. iii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 33 ), that is, hath “showed forth undeniably.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Which indeed the expression seems to me to hint at even in this place, for “the Father hath sealed,” is nothing else than “hath declared,” “hath revealed by His testimony.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: He in fact declared Himself too, but since He was speaking to Jews, He brought forward the testimony of the Father.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Learn we then, beloved, to ask of God the things which it is meet for us to ask of Him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For those other things, those, I mean, which belong to this life, whichever way they may fall out, can do us no injury; for if we be rich, it is here only that we shall enjoy our luxury; and if we fall into poverty, we shall suffer nothing terrible.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For neither the splendors nor the pains of the present life have much power in respect either of despondency or pleasure, they are contemptible, and slip away very swiftly.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Wherefore they are called “a way,” with reason, because they pass away, and by their very nature do not long endure, but the things which are to come endure eternally, both those of punishment and those of the Kingdom.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Let us then in regard of these things use much diligence to avoid the first and to choose the last.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For what is the advantage of this world’s luxury?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: To-day it is, and to-morrow it is not; to-day a bright flower, to-morrow scattered dust; to-day a burning fire, to-morrow smouldering ashes.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But spiritual things are not so, they ever remain shining and blooming, and becoming brighter every day.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: That wealth never perishes, never departs, never ceases, never brings with it care or envy or blame, destroys not the body, corrupts not the soul, is without ill will, heaps not up malice; all which things attend on the other kind of wealth.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: That honor lifts not men into folly, doth not make them puffed up, never ceases nor is dimmed.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Again, the rest and delight of heaven endureth continually, ever being immovable and immortal, one cannot find its end or limit.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: This life then let us desire, for if we do so we shall make no account of present things, but shall despise and mock at them all, and though one should bid us enter into kingly halls, we shall not while we have this hope choose to do so; yet nothing (earthly) seems more near to happiness than such a permission; but to those who are possessed by love of heaven, even this seems little and mean, and worthy of no account.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Nothing which comes to an end is to be much desired; whatever ceases, and to-day is and tomorrow is not, even though it be very great, yet seems to be very little and contemptible.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Then let us not cling to fleeting things which slip away and depart, but to those which are enduring and immovable.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: To which may we all attain, through the grace and lovingkindness of our Lord Jesus Christ, by whom and with whom, to the Father and the Holy Ghost, be glory, now and ever and world without end.
